aucmat 0.2.0

Simulate a biomarker matrix with target AUCs and a parametrized correlation structure

Source: R/simulate-auc-matrix.R
simulate_auc_matrix.Rd

Generates correlated biomarkers from a class-conditional multivariate normal: X | Y = 1 ~ MVN(mu_1, R) and X | Y = 0 ~ MVN(mu_0, R), with the same p x p correlation matrix R in both classes and a per-biomarker mean shift mu_1 - mu_0 calibrated in closed form from target_aucs (the equal-variance binormal relationship also used by generate_data_analytical()). R is built from a named structure instead of requiring a hand-supplied matrix, and feasibility is reported as structured diagnostics instead of a bare warning.

Usage

simulate_auc_matrix(
  n = NULL,
  prevalence = NULL,
  y = NULL,
  positive = NULL,
  outcome_mode = c("exact", "bernoulli"),
  target_aucs,
  correlation = NULL,
  structure = c("user", "exchangeable", "ar1", "block"),
  block_sizes = NULL,
  rho_within = NULL,
  rho_between = NULL,
  feasibility = c("error", "nearest"),
  seed = NULL,
  verify = TRUE
)

Arguments

n

Number of observations (generated-outcome mode).

prevalence

Proportion of positive class, in (0, 1) (generated-outcome mode).

y

Binary outcome vector (supplied-outcome mode). Accepted inputs are logical, numeric 0/1, factor, or character with exactly two non-missing classes.

positive

Optional positive class label when y is a factor or character (supplied-outcome mode).

outcome_mode

"exact" (default) or "bernoulli". Only used when generating y from n/prevalence.

target_aucs

Numeric vector of target AUC values, each in (0, 1).

correlation

For structure = "user", the full p x p target correlation matrix. For "exchangeable" or "ar1", a single correlation value in (-1, 1). Unused for "block".

structure

One of "user" (default), "exchangeable", "ar1", or "block".

block_sizes

Integer vector of block sizes summing to length(target_aucs). Required when structure = "block".

rho_within, rho_between

Within- and between-block correlations. Required when structure = "block".

feasibility

"error" (default) or "nearest". When the requested p x p correlation matrix is not positive definite, "error" stops with a structured aucmat_infeasible_targets condition; "nearest" projects to the nearest positive-semidefinite matrix and proceeds, reporting the adjustment.

seed

Optional integer seed.

verify

Logical. If TRUE (default), empirically verify achieved AUCs and correlations on the generated sample.

Value

An object of class aucmat_simulation, a list with components data, target_aucs, achieved_aucs, requested_correlation, achieved_correlation, mean_shift, Sigma, feasibility, n, prevalence, seed. achieved_correlation is the within-class correlation (each class centred on its own empirical mean before correlating), matching the scope of requested_correlation. The raw pooled correlation of data$X1, data$X2, ... (ignoring truth) will be systematically higher whenever the corresponding AUCs are above 0.5, because mixing two subpopulations with different means inflates pooled correlation independently of the within-class dependence structure.

Details

Exactly one of (y, positive) or (n, prevalence) must be supplied. Supplying y retains its exact values, class counts, and row order. Generating n/prevalence defaults to exact rounded class counts with randomised row order (outcome_mode = "exact"); pass outcome_mode = "bernoulli" for independent Bernoulli draws with random class counts. Because the outcome is fixed before any biomarker is drawn, both modes deliver the outcome's class counts exactly – no biomarker is generated by thresholding a latent variable.

Examples

# \donttest{
set.seed(1)
sim <- simulate_auc_matrix(
  n = 500, prevalence = 0.3,
  target_aucs = c(0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.65),
  correlation = 0.4, structure = "exchangeable"
)
data.frame(target = sim$target_aucs, achieved = round(sim$achieved_aucs, 3))
#>    target achieved
#> X1   0.90    0.887
#> X2   0.80    0.794
#> X3   0.70    0.688
#> X4   0.65    0.641
# }
